mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls signal expanding household income and aggregate consumption demand, raising expected corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth does not coincide with significantly positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, persistent joblessness constrains wage growth and consumer spending, eroding corporate earnings expectations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE > 8.5% over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate and decreases in nonfarm payrolls indicate a coordinated labor market contraction that amplifies recession risk beyond either signal in isolation, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising UNRATE and falling PAYEMS fails to produce negative 20-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In an elevated inflation regime where CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 2.5%, month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate raise equity discount rates and compress valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rate hikes in high-inflation months do not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that reduces near-term earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to produce negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, which boosts corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth does not align with significantly positive S&P 500 20-day forward returns (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0% annualized confirms that aggregate demand is expanding sufficiently to sustain corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth exceeding 2.0% annualized does not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered quarters over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.3% signals an inflation surprise that raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL increases exceeding 0.3% do not coincide with negative 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of occurrences over a 2-year rolling window.
